hawkish_phrases: the current stance of monetary policy is appropriate
a few participants raised the concern that keeping interest rates low over a long period might encourage excessive risk-taking
various participants were concerned that indicators were suggesting that the level of longer-term inflation expectations was too low
inflation had averaged less than 2 percent over the past several years even as resource utilization had increased
maintaining the current stance of monetary policy would give the Committee some time to assess the full effects
dovish_phrases: business fixed investment and exports remained weak
overall inflation and inflation for items other than food and energy were running below 2 percent
global developments, related to both persistent uncertainty regarding international trade and weakness in economic growth abroad, continued to pose some risks
maintaining the current stance of policy for a time could be helpful for cushioning the economy from the global developments
summary: The FOMC unanimously held rates at 1.5-1.75% with statement language explicitly judging the current stance appropriate, while continuing to cite global developments and muted inflation pressures as factors to monitor, reflecting a mildly hawkish hold after three consecutive cuts earlier in 2019.
